mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and robust economic expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate signals a shift towards monetary tightening, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, compressing corporate valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.1% over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If, after a triggered event, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong retail sales growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate robust consumer demand and a healthy labor market, which together drive corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.1pp
failure_condition: If, when both conditions are met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, strong housing starts signal a broad economic rebound and increased consumer confidence, which positively impacts overall economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 1000 (thousands of units) and remains above for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, during a post-recession period, the trigger is met but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and business activity, which generally translates to stronger corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 performance over short horizons.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 6-month period
failure_condition: If, after a triggered event, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with slowing retail sales growth indicates potential stagflationary pressures, where consumers face higher prices but have less purchasing power, leading to a squeeze on corporate margins and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 2%
failure_condition: If, when both conditions are met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and equity market appreciation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent quarter-over-quarter growth but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
